repo_prior_art.cite: stat_ate_overlap_decay + overlap-tail phase bookkeeping, but for weak-overlap ATE risk rather than CCOT PRTE endpoint inference.

published_prior_art.cite: DaouiaFlorensSimar2010FrontierEVT + boundary-tail normalizations for frontier estimation, not CCOT PRTE endpoint localization.

TanBlanchetSyrgkanis2026PRTEOT gives continuous-instrument rates; FangSantos2019DirectionalInference gives generic directional-differentiability inference; neither gives this boundary-tail CCOT PRTE local experiment.

TanBlanchetSyrgkanis2026PRTEOT gives continuous-instrument nonparametric convergence rates, while FranguridiLiu2025RegularizedOTPartialID studies regularized OT inference rather than an unregularized CCOT boundary endpoint.

repo_prior_art.cite: stat_ate_overlap_decay + overlap-tail minimax machinery, but not PRTE-OT honest-CI adaptation across alpha classes.

published_prior_art.cite: CaiLow2005AdaptiveCI and RobinsVanderVaart2006AdaptiveSets give abstract adaptation obstructions, not a CCOT PRTE two-alpha construction.
ImbensManski2004PartialCI and Stoye2009MorePartialCI are regular partial-ID endpoint CI templates; this statement is only a sanity reduction.
FangSantos2019DirectionalInference and FranguridiLiu2025RegularizedOTPartialID motivate nonsmooth and OT calibration but do not construct fixed-alpha honest CIs for this endpoint.

soundness_flags.label: thm:local-experiment
soundness_flags.one_line: The process H_{n,P} is indexed on [0,1], but for alpha <= 1 the value at u=0 has infinite chi-weighted B^{-1} variance, so H_{n,P}(0), ell_infinity([0,1]) tightness, and the displayed covariance kernel are not well-defined.

soundness_flags.label: thm:local-experiment
soundness_flags.one_line: The claimed covariance-ratio convergence uniformly on [0,1]^2 fails at and near u=0 in the alpha=1 and alpha<1 regimes; the statement needs a restricted index set or a different boundary compactification.

soundness_flags.label: ass:strict-overlap / prop:strict-overlap-reduction
soundness_flags.one_line: Since B is defined as distance from the lower support infimum, the condition B >= b_so almost surely makes the strict-overlap class empty or vacuous under ordinary support conventions; strict overlap should lower-bound the propensity itself against a fixed external boundary.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:active-face-gap
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The endpoint limit-law claims are conditional on a unique active OT face with a boundary-scaled dual gap.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The headline inference claims are bound to the unique-active-face subclass P_n(alpha).

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:boundary-loading-tail
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The alpha phase rate is conditional on chi_P-weighted boundary tails being comparable to raw boundary tails.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The phase diagram applies only when the active-face derivative loading neither vanishes nor explodes at the propensity edge.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:regular-linearization
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The regular interior component away from the boundary is assumed pathwise differentiable.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The proposed limit law characterizes the added boundary component conditional on an assumed regular Gaussian interior component.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: oeq:boundary-honest-ci
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: A fixed-alpha honest CI attaining the oracle length is explicitly left open.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The current kernel supplies the lower-bound obstruction and target handle, not a complete adaptive inference theory.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Redefine the boundary score process on a domain that excludes the singular u=0 point, or add a compactified/process-level definition whose covariance is finite in the alpha <= 1 regimes.

Replace ass:strict-overlap with a non-vacuous strict-overlap condition on the propensity support, such as p(Z,X) bounded away from the policy-relevant lower propensity boundary by an external constant.
State the no-adaptation lower-bound route with an explicit two-point or modulus construction target: tail exponents, endpoint separation, and bounded product-divergence scale.
Add a boundary corollary separating alpha=1 pointwise convergence from uniform convergence near u=0, since the log transition is exactly where a referee will test the normalization.

recommended_next_step: Continue revise on this angle: novelty clears the field floor, but the boundary-process domain and strict-overlap sanity class need semantic repair before Stage 0.
